rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE L. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Approve The Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as no petition has been filed to request approval of a final account of the deceased executor's administration (or a final report on waiver of account) and exoneration of the deceased executor's bond. Other issues include: 1) Each specific cash beneficiary must also waive the account, as no 7% interest required by Probate Code section 12003 appears to have been paid. See also section 10954(a)(2). 2) A copy of letters issued to David Long as personal representative of the estate of the Nancy Bass must be filed before his waiver of account can be accepted. 3) No receipt signed by the successor personal representative to confirm the estate assets on hand is on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES HEARRING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service was verified on 5/9/23, before the date notice was mailed on 5/10/23. (2) The will named beneficiaries other than just the "heirs" named in paragraph 4 of this report. (3) When did Margaret J. Johnson die and how did she leave her interest to Vinson E. Johnson? (Did she have a trust? Was a personal representative appointed for her estate? Did she assign her interest in writing and, if so, when was that filed?) (4) The petition does not set forth the addresses of the persons entitled to notice. Notice was mailed to some at addresses other than those to which notice of prior hearings was mailed. (5) Paragraph 3 of the petition mentions the sale of the property in Fresno County but does not say when it was sold and for how much or what the net sale proceeds were. (6) The real property interest at Bridgeview Avenue in San Francisco was ârecently inventoriedâ over a year ago, on 2/17/22. (7) Has that real property interest in San Francisco been sold (when and for how much) and the proceeds already distributed, as paragraph 5 seems to indicate? (8) This report of status does not meet all of the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. Additional disclosures must be made. (9) It appears that the executor no longer resides in California; bond is required of a non-resident personal representative. She must promptly file an ex parte petition for an order setting bond. [CRC 7.204 and LRSF 14.16 F.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 8/28/23. If all of these issues are not fully addressed prior to 8/28/23, an appearance will likely be required on 9/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILSON PENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/1/2023. Verified supplement addressing the issues: (1) further allegation if the request for funeral expenses of $48,851 paid by aunt is or is not verified claim and its reasonableness. Administrator who was authorized to exercise full IAEA is seeking the court's approval for said reimbursement in Paragraph #20; however, the court has no further information such as the reasonableness and verified/non-verified claim. Prayer does not include the request, either. Need clarification. (2) Need allegation that the estate funds are fully covered by FDIC. Appears cash funds in on account exceeds $250K. (3) Proposed Order to be submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY EVERETT MOATS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/15/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 05/23/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No DE-120 form (including proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. 4) No inventory and appraisal is on file. 5) The financial account statement verifying the ending cash balance must be filed (see LRSF 14.34(A)) with a declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A). 6) The beginning date of the account must be the date of the decedent's death. LRSF 14.34. 7) The accounting format and each schedule must comply with Probate Code sections 1061-1063. 8) Allegations required by section 1064(a)(1)-(5) must be provided. 9) Has the petitioner delivered all the estate assets to the successor personal representatives? If so, a signed receipt must be filed before the petitioner's bond can be exonerated on this petition. The descriptions of the assets on the signed receipt must match those on the schedule of assets at the end of the account period.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD LASALLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although neither the petition caption nor the prayer included the request to withhold $20,000 as a reserve for fiduciary income tax returns (and, presumably the taxes themselves) and closing expenses, and the report did not fully disclose the results of the actions Executor took under his IAEA authority (e.g., did not disclose for how much the real property and the automobile were sold and what the net sale proceeds were). As the sole beneficiary is an organization, it is assumed that it can advocate for itself better than an individual could. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: âOrder for Final Distribution.â [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MERVYN WYNN MEMORIAL TRAVEL FUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Fourteenth Account And Report Of De La Salle Institute And Petition For Settlement Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/12/23. (No earlier availability.) There were 18,036.192 shares of the fund on hand with a "cost" (carry value) of $217,382.82 as of 4/30/19. During this accounting period, 655.116 shares having a carry value of $7,816.73 were sold. How are there 17640.254 shares with a carry value of $232,907.96 on hand as of 4/30/22? If Schedule M of the amended accounting is meant to show this, it does not; subtotals or even totals would be helpful. Schedule G of the 13th accounting is a helpful template of how to show changes in the form of assets. A supplement to the amended petition must be filed to address the issues with the accounting and a courtesy copy of it must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/1/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The grace period for providing notice without the instructions for how to appear remotely has expired. Please make a note of it. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSEMARY ANNE GILBERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/08/2023, as the following issues (last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 05/22/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) Re the DE 121 form: i) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. ii) The proof of service does not show the country name as part of the addresses of Stephen Lawson and Elaine Lawson. 2) The petition caption: The IAEA box must be checked. 3) Item 2d of the petition: At least one box must be checked. 4) Item 3d: If the estate value is $0, please explain why the petitioner is opening this probate. 5) Item 3f(2): Are the will and both codicils not self-proving? 6) Item 3h(3) must be checked. 7) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 8) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 9) Item 5b: At least one box must be checked. 10) Item 8: i) The petitioner's address must be listed. ii) All devisees and executors (including alternate executors) named in the will and each codicil must be listed. iii) Please provide a family tree with the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and all other known relatives. iv) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts made to identify and locate the decedent's heirs entitled to notice. 11) Item 9: The number of attachment pages must be specified. An appearance may be required on 08/08/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, if no satisfactory progress to address the issues is shown.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL A. MAGIDSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Where is the declination of the other named co-executor, Judith Marx? (2) If the decedent was survived by issue of deceased parents, who are the surviving siblings or their surviving issue and who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? [LRSF 14.18 C.] (3) If first cousins are the nearest surviving kin, then item 6b of the petition is incorrect. The decedent's predeceased parents, grandparents, and aunts/uncles must be identified and their dates of death set forth. (4) Notice must include the language regarding the CURRENT, correct remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 8/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Waiver of bond is not necessary when a will waives bond. This Court no longer requires the local form Declaration of Real Property.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL POTTER PECKHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/1/23. (1) Petition to affirm the date codicil in #3.f.(2); (2) Notice to be directly given to Peter H. unless the court has dispensed with such notice. CRC(c)(2); (3) Proof or supporting documents showing interests of Peter H. and Robert C. P being represented by another person (i.e., Letters of Conservator, Power of Attorney etc.; (4) Notice to Art Gallery of Greater Victoria; and (5) Proposed order Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TATSURO IWAMOTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no courtesy copy of the proof of service filed on 5/16/23 was received by the examiner by 6/8/23. Status of closing the estate is set for 7/9/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 7/9/24. Appearance will be required on 7/9/24 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOANNE HOPE JEPSON, M.D. 2001 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continue to 8/1/2023. (1) Counsel for petitioner needs to sign petition. (2) Petition to allege how the settlor could have intended to transfer title to her IRA to her trust while Federal law appears to require an IRA to be held by an individual and Federal law treats a transfer of an IRA to a revocable living trust as prohibited transactions and commingling. (3) Decedent was survived by relatives. Petition to identify who are the decedent's heirs-at-law, including the ancestor(s) under the next generation would have taken. (4) If any of the above persons in (3) is not noticed, the person must be given with Notice of Hearing. (5) Merrill Lynch's Response needs to be reviewed (filing pending). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES A. MCKENZIE AND MARINA MCKENZIE 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions;
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) The only correct form of Notice of Hearing for trust petitions filed in Probate is either DE-120 or DE-115. Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Deadline is 9/8/23 for delivering the proposed order and endorsed filed courtesy copies of the petition and proof of service of the correct/complete Notice of Hearing to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORINE JEW-HOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/18/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/19/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) Item 3a of the petition: The decedent died more than 5 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. 2) Item 3d(4)-(6) must be completed if the decedent owned real property, in addition to (2)(a) & (3). 3) Item 3g(2)(b) must be checked instead of 3g(2)(a). 4) Is the decedent's 20% interest in the real property described at item 3d separate property, community property, or both? 5) Does the decedent's surviving spouse intend to make an election under Probate Code section 13502 to probate his share, rather than proceeding with a spousal property petition?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NELLIE JEW HUE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/18/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/19/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) Item 3a of the petition: The decedent died more than 5 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. 2) Item 3c: Is probate administration for the decedent's estate pending in Nevada? If so, please identify the personal representative appointed by Nevada court. 3) Item 3d(4)-(6) must be completed if the decedent owned real property, in addition to (2)(a) & (3). 4) Attachment 8: Kevin Hue's address (state) is different from his address listed on the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form. Notice must be given to the correct address at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a signed waiver of notice on file. A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must also be attached to and served with the DE-121 form (telephone number for a zoom appearance stated on the attachment filed on 06/08/2023 must be corrected).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID WARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Notwithstanding the lack of courtesy copies, the petition was reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] The instructions that were mailed must be attached to the proof of service so that it is clear that the correct instructions were served with the notice. (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 8/28/23 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE ANDREWS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Contined 8/1/2023. Premature for examiner's review as no other required documents have been filed other than Petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FREEDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of publication has been filed. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) The top of page 10 of the copy of the will attached to the petition was cut off. A full copy of the will must be filed. (5) Did the testator leave any written directions regarding the disposition of tangible personal property? (See Gifts in Section V of the will.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 8/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS P. ALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. Attachment 2c not needed. Status of closing the estate is set for August 20, 2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALDINE CLARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/18/2023 (no earlier date available). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 06/12/2023. Note: The DE-121 form filed on 06/08/2023 shows a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) was served less than 15 days before this hearing.
